query: What are my rights regarding minimum wage?
response:
You have the right to receive minimum wages as declared by the Provincial Government, and your employer cannot pay you less than the minimum rate of wages [Punjab Domestic Workers Act, Section 19 — "No employer shall pay any domestic worker wages at a rate lower than the rate declared under this Act to be the minimum rate of wages for such domestic worker"]. The minimum wages shall be applicable to all workers, including time-rated workers, piece-rated workers, and workers engaged on a contract basis [KPK Minimum Wages Notification, Section 2 — "the minimum wages shall be applicable to time rated workers including temporary workers, piece-rated workers and workers engaged on contract basis as per sub-section (2) of section 5 of the Act ibid"]. 

Note: This is general legal information, not legal advice. For your specific situation, consult a qualified lawyer or contact your provincial Labour Department.

query: how much is maternity leave in sindh
retrieved_laws: West Pakistan Shops and Establishments Ordinance S15
response:
In Sindh, the maternity leave is 12 weeks. 
According to the Maternity Benefit Ordinance [Maternity Benefit Ordinance, Section 4 — "Every woman employed in an establishment is entitled to maternity benefit at the rate of her last paid wages for: - 6 weeks immediately before delivery - 6 weeks after delivery - Total: 12 weeks of maternity benefit"], every woman employed in an establishment is entitled to maternity benefit at the rate of her last paid wages for 6 weeks immediately before delivery and 6 weeks after delivery, totaling 12 weeks of maternity benefit. 
Note: This is general legal information, not legal advice. For your specific situation, consult a qualified lawyer or contact your provincial Labour Department.

query: how muhc maternity leave do i get if im on a contract
retrieved_laws: KPK Shops and Establishment Act S15
response:
As a contract worker, you are entitled to 12 weeks of maternity benefit, which includes 6 weeks before delivery and 6 weeks after delivery [Maternity Benefit Ordinance, Section 4 — "Every woman employed in an establishment is entitled to maternity benefit at the rate of her last paid wages for: - 6 weeks immediately before delivery - 6 weeks after delivery - Total: 12 weeks of maternity benefit"]. 

TheMaternity Benefit Ordinance applies to all establishments, including those with contract workers [Maternity Benefit Ordinance, Section 7 — "it shall not be lawful for her employer to give her notice of dismissal during such absence"], and it is the employer's responsibility to pay the maternity benefit for the entire 12-week period [Maternity Benefit Ordinance, Section 5 — "Employer shall pay maternity benefit for 12 weeks total"].

Note: This is general legal information, not legal advice. For your specific situation, consult a qualified lawyer or contact your provincial Labour Department.

query: how muhc maternity leave do i get if im on a daily wage job

Punjab Shops and Establishments Ordinance S15
response:
As a daily wage worker, you are entitled to 12 weeks of maternity benefit, which includes 6 weeks before delivery and 6 weeks after delivery, as stated in the Maternity Benefit Ordinance [Maternity Benefit Ordinance, Section 4 — "Every woman employed in an establishment is entitled to maternity benefit at the rate of her last paid wages for: - 6 weeks immediately before delivery - 6 weeks after delivery - Total: 12 weeks of maternity benefit"]. 

This benefit is applicable to all establishments throughout Pakistan [Maternity Benefit Ordinance, Section 7 — "it shall not be lawful for her employer to give her notice of dismissal during such absence"], and the employer shall pay the maternity benefit for the total period of 12 weeks [Maternity Benefit Ordinance, Section 5 — "Employer shall pay maternity benefit for 12 weeks total"].

query: What are my rights regarding workplace discrimination?
retrieved_laws: KPK Shops and Establishment Act S36
KPK Payment of Wages Act S26
response:
You have the right to be protected against discrimination in the workplace based on various factors such as gender, religion, sect, colour, caste, creed, ethnic background, and political affiliation. 
As stated in the KPK Shops and Establishment Act, Section 36 — "No discrimination shall be made on the basis of gender, religion, sect, colour, caste, creed, ethnic back ground and political affiliation with regard to the observance of this Act." 
Additionally, the KPK Payment of Wages Act, Section 26, states — "There shall be no discrimination on the basis of gender, religion, sect, colour, cast, creed, ethnic back ground in the wages and other benefits for work of equal value."
Note: This is general legal information, not legal advice. For your specific situation, consult a qualified lawyer or contact your provincial Labour Department.

query: My boss fired me because I got pregnant. Is this legal?
response:
Your employer cannot fire you simply because you got pregnant, as this would be a form of discriminatory dismissal. According to the Maternity Benefit Ordinance, Section 7 — "it shall not be lawful for her employer to give her notice of dismissal during such absence or on such a day that the notice will expire during such absence." This provision suggests that employers are restricted from dismissing pregnant workers during their maternity leave or in a manner that would deprive them of their maternity benefits. 

Note: This is general legal information, not legal advice. For your specific situation, consult a qualified lawyer or contact your provincial Labour Department.

query: میری تنخواہ نہیں ملی، میں کیا کروں؟
retrieved_laws: Industrial and Commercial Employment Standing Orders Ordinance S10
KPK Industrial and Commercial Employment Standing Orders Act S11
Balochistan Payment of Wages Act S6
response:
آپ کی تنخواہ نہیں ملنے کے معاملے میں،Industrial and Commercial Employment Standing Orders Ordinance کے سیکشن 10 کے انڈر [1.2] میں کہا गयا ہے کہ "اللہ.workmen کو ان کے کام کے حقیقی دن سے پہلے 7ویں یا 10ویں دن کے اندر تنخواہ دی جائے گی" [Industrial and Commercial Employment Standing Orders Ordinance, Section 10 — "All workmen shall be paid wages on a working day before the expiry of the 7th or 10th day after the last day of the wage period"]۔
یہ بھی کہا گیا ہے کہ jika تنخواہ نہیں دی گئی ہے تو اس کا ذکر notice boards پر کردیا جائے گا، جیسا کہ [1.1] میں کہا گیا ہے کہ "Any wages, due to the workman but not paid on the usual pay day on account of their being unclaimed, shall be paid by the employer on an unclaimed wage pay day in each week" [Industrial and Commercial Employment Standing Orders Ordinance, Section 10 — "Any wages, due to the workman but not paid on the usual pay day on account of their being unclaimed, shall be paid by the employer on an unclaimed wage pay day in each week"]。
اگر آپ کی تنخواہ نہیں ملی ہے تو آپ کو اپنے ملازم کے پاس kontakt کرنا چاہئے اور ان سے اپنی تنخواہ کے بارے میں پوچھنا چاہئے۔

نوٹ: یہ عمومی قانونی معلومات ہے، قانونی مشورہ نہیں۔ اپنی مخصوص صورتحال کے لیے کسی قابل وکیل سے ملیں یا اپنے صوبائی لیبر ڈیپارٹمنٹ سے رابطہ کریں۔

query: How many days of annual leave am I entitled to in KPK?
retrieved_laws: KPK Shops and Establishment Act S15
KPK Shops and Establishment Act S16
KPK Shops and Establishment Act S38
response:
You are entitled to fourteen days of annual leave with full wages after continuous employment in an establishment for a period of twelve months in KPK [KPK Shops and Establishment Act, Section 15 — "Every employee shall be allowed leave with full wages for a period of fourteen days after continuous employment in an establishment, whether in the same or different capacities, for a period of twelve months."]. This leave can be accumulated, but when the total leave due to an employee under this section amounts to thirty days, no further accumulation of or addition to such leave will be permissible [KPK Shops and Establishment Act, Section 15 — "provided that when the total leave due to an employee under this section amounts to thirty days, no further accumulation of or addition to such leave will be permissible;"]. 

Note: This is general legal information, not legal advice. For your specific situation, consult a qualified lawyer or contact your provincial Labour Department.

query: My boss fired me because I got pregnant. Is this legal?
response:
It is not lawful for an employer to give a woman notice of dismissal during her absence due to pregnancy or on a day that the notice will expire during such absence [Maternity Benefit Ordinance, Section 7 — "it shall not be lawful for her employer to give her notice of dismissal during such absence or on such a day that the notice will expire during such absence"]. Additionally, if a woman is given notice of dismissal without sufficient cause by her employer within six months before delivery, it may deprive her of her maternity benefits [Maternity Benefit Ordinance, Section 7 — "(2) (a) No notice of dismissal given without sufficient cause by an employer to a woman within a period of six months before delivery shall have the effect of depriving her of any maternity benefit to which but for such notice she may have become entitled under this Ordinance"].

Note: This is general legal information, not legal advice. For your specific situation, consult a qualified lawyer or contact your provincial Labour Department.

query: Should I quit my job or file a complaint?
retrieved_laws: KPK Shops and Establishment Act S20
West Pakistan Shops and Establishments Ordinance S19
Punjab Shops and Establishments Ordinance S19
KPK Industrial and Commercial Employment Standing Orders Act S17
Industrial and Commercial Employment Standing Orders Ordinance S11
response:
It's recommended that you file a complaint rather than quitting your job, as quitting may result in loss of entitlement to notice or pay in lieu thereof [KPK Shops and Establishment Act, Section 20 — "one month's notice in writing shall be given either by the employer or by the employee and in lieu of notice, one month’s wages calculated on the basis of average of wages earned during the preceding three months shall be paid or forfeited, as the case may be."]. 

If you are a permanent employee, you are entitled to one month's notice in writing or one month's wages in lieu of notice [West Pakistan Shops and Establishments Ordinance, Section 19 — "For terminating employment of a permanent employee, one month’s notice in writing shall be given either by the employer or by the employee and in lieu of notice, one month’s wages calculated on the basis of average of wages earned during the preceding three months shall be paid."]. 

Similarly, if you are a worker under the KPK Industrial and Commercial Employment Standing Orders Act, you are entitled to one month's notice or one month's wages in lieu of notice [KPK Industrial and Commercial Employment Standing Orders Act, Section 17 — "For terminating employment of a permanent worker, for any reason other than misconduct, one month‟s notice shall be given either by the employer or the worker."]. 

Note: This is general legal information, not legal advice. For your specific situation, consult a qualified lawyer or contact your provincial Labour Department.

query: What is the minimum wage in Sindh for unskilled workers in 2024?
response:
The minimum wage in Sindh for unskilled workers in 2024 is Rs. 37,000/- per month. 
This is as per the Sindh Minimum Wage Notification, which states that the minimum rates of wages for unskilled adult and adolescent workers employed in all industrial and commercial establishments in Sindh shall be Rs. 37,000/- per month, with effect from 1st July, 2024 [Sindh Minimum Wage Notification (Unskilled), Section: 1 — "the minimum rates of the wages @ Rs. 37,000/- per month, for Unskilled Adult and Adolescent Workers employed in all Industrial & Commercial Establishments in Sindh subject to following conditions, with effect from 1° July, 2024."].
Note: This is general legal information, not legal advice. For your specific situation, consult a qualified lawyer or contact your provincial Labour Department.

query: How many weeks of maternity leave am I entitled to?
retrieved_laws: KPK Shops and Establishment Act S15
Punjab Shops and Establishments Ordinance S14
response:
You are entitled to 12 weeks of maternity leave. 
This is because, as stated in the Maternity Benefit Ordinance, "Every woman employed in an establishment is entitled to maternity benefit at the rate of her last paid wages for: - 6 weeks immediately before delivery - 6 weeks after delivery - Total: 12 weeks of maternity benefit" [Maternity Benefit Ordinance, Section 4 — "Every woman employed in an establishment is entitled to maternity benefit at the rate of her last paid wages for: - 6 weeks immediately before delivery - 6 weeks after delivery - Total: 12 weeks of maternity benefit"]. 
Additionally, the total benefit period is also specified as 12 weeks [Maternity Benefit Ordinance, Section 5 — "TOTAL BENEFIT PERIOD (Section 5): Employer shall pay maternity benefit for 12 weeks total."].
Note: This is general legal information, not legal advice. For your specific situation, consult a qualified lawyer or contact your provincial Labour Department.

query: My boss fired me because I got pregnant. Is this legal?
response:
Your employer cannot lawfully dismiss you due to pregnancy, as stated in the Maternity Benefit Ordinance. According to the Maternity Benefit Ordinance, Section 7 — "it shall not be lawful for her employer to give her notice of dismissal during such absence or on such a day that the notice will expire during such absence." This provision applies when a woman is absent from work in accordance with the provisions of the Ordinance, implying that pregnancy-related leave is protected. 

Note: This is general legal information, not legal advice. For your specific situation, consult a qualified lawyer or contact your provincial Labour Department.

query: How many days of annual leave am I entitled to in KPK?
retrieved_laws: KPK Shops and Establishment Act S15
KPK Shops and Establishment Act S16
KPK Shops and Establishment Act S38
response:
You are entitled to 14 days of annual leave in KPK. 
Every employee shall be allowed leave with full wages for a period of fourteen days after continuous employment in an establishment, whether in the same or different capacities, for a period of twelve months [KPK Shops and Establishment Act, Section 15 — "Every employee shall be allowed leave with full wages for a period of fourteen days after continuous employment in an establishment, whether in the same or different capacities, for a period of twelve months"]. 
Note: This is general legal information, not legal advice. For your specific situation, consult a qualified lawyer or contact your provincial Labour Department.

query: How do I file a harassment complaint at my workplace?
retrieved_laws: Protection Against Harassment of Women at Workplace Act S8
Protection Against Harassment of Women at Workplace Act S11
Protection Against Harassment of Women at Workplace Act S11
response:
To file a harassment complaint at your workplace, you can prefer a complaint either to the Ombudsman or the Inquiry Committee [Protection Against Harassment of Women at Workplace Act, Section 8 — "Any employee shall have the option to prefer a complaint either to the Ombudsman or the Inquiry Committee"]. The Ombudsman shall within 3 days of receiving a complaint issue a written show cause notice to the accused [Protection Against Harassment of Women at Workplace Act, Section 8 — "The Ombudsman shall within 3 days of receiving a complaint issue a written show cause notice to the accused"]. 

It is the responsibility of the employer to ensure implementation of the Protection Against Harassment of Women at Workplace Act, including but not limited to incorporating the Code of Conduct for protection against harassment at the workplace as a part of their management policy [Protection Against Harassment of Women at Workplace Act, Section 11 — "It shall be the responsibility of the employer to ensure implementation of this Act, including but not limited to incorporate the Code of Conduct for protection against harassment at the workplace as a part of their management policy"].

Note: This is general legal information, not legal advice. For your specific situation, consult a qualified lawyer or contact your provincial Labour Department.

query: What is the overtime rate in Islam?
response:
The overtime rate is not directly related to Islam, but according to Pakistani labour law, specifically the Factories Act, the overtime rate is twice the ordinary rate of pay [Factories Act, Section 47 — "he shall be entitled in respect of the overtime worked to pay at the rate of twice his ordinary rate of pay"]. The ordinary rate of pay is defined as all remuneration capable of being expressed in terms of money which would, if the terms of the contract of employment, express or implied, were fulfilled, be payable to a worker in respect of his employment or of work done in such employment [Factories Act, Section 47 — "‘ordinary rate of pay’ means all remuneration capable of being expressed in terms of money which would, if the terms of the contract of employment, express or implied, were fulfilled, be payable to a worker in respect of his employment or of work done in such employment"].

Note: This is general legal information, not legal advice. For your specific situation, consult a qualified lawyer or contact your provincial Labour Department.

query: What are my rights if I'm being terminated?
retrieved_laws: Industrial and Commercial Employment Standing Orders Ordinance S19
KPK Industrial and Commercial Employment Standing Orders Act S4
West Pakistan Shops and Establishments Ordinance S19
Punjab Shops and Establishments Ordinance S19
KPK Shops and Establishment Act S20
response:
If you're being terminated, you are entitled to a service certificate as per the Industrial and Commercial Employment Standing Orders Ordinance [Industrial and Commercial Employment Standing Orders Ordinance, Section 19 — "Every permanent workman shall be entitled to a service certificate at the time of his dismissal, discharge, retrenchment or retirement from service."]. Additionally, for terminating employment of a permanent employee, one month’s notice in writing shall be given either by the employer or by the employee and in lieu of notice, one month’s wages calculated on the basis of average of wages earned during the preceding three months shall be paid [West Pakistan Shops and Establishments Ordinance, Section 19 — "For terminating employment of a permanent employee, one month’s notice in writing shall be given either by the employer or by the employee and in lieu of notice, one month’s wages calculated on the basis of average of wages earned during the preceding three months shall be paid."].

Note: This is general legal information, not legal advice. For your specific situation, consult a qualified lawyer or contact your provincial Labour Department.

query: لیبر انسپکٹر سے دستاویزات کیسے چھپائی جا سکتی ہیں؟
response:
لیبر انسپکٹر سے دستاویزات چھپانے کے بارے میں کوئی واضح قانونی حکم نہیں مل سکا۔ میں نے دستاویزات کو چھپانے کی قانونی کارروائی کے بارے میں کوئی معلومات نہیں پائی۔ 
مائن ایکٹ کے سیکشن 9 کے مطابق، چیف انسپکٹر یا انسپکٹر کو کوئی بھی معلومات رازداری کے ساتھ رکھنی ہوتی ہے اور اسے اس کی مرضی کے خلاف نہیں بتایا جا سکتا [مائن ایکٹ، سیکشن 9 — "چیف انسپکٹر یا انسپکٹر کو کوئی بھی معلومات رازداری کے ساتھ رکھنی ہوتی ہے اور اسے اس کی مرضی کے خلاف نہیں بتایا جا سکتا"]۔
لیکن یہ法律ی حکم لیبر انسپکٹر سے دستاویزات چھپانے کے بارے میں واضح نہیں ہے۔

query: سندھ میں مزدوروں کے لیے کم از کم اجرت کیا ہے؟
response:
سندھ میں مزدوروں کے لیے کم از کم اجرت روپے 37,000/- فی ماہ ہے [Sindh Minimum Wage Notification (Unskilled), Section: 1 — "GOVERNMENT OF SINDH LABOUR & HUMAN RESOURCES DEPARTMENT  Karachi dated the 22"! October, 2024 NOTIFICATION  No. SO(L-II)13-03/2016-I: -In pursuance of Section 4(1) of the Sindh Minimum Wages Act, 2015 and in exercise of the power conferred by sub-section (1)(a) of Section 6 of Sindh Minimum Wages Act, 2015, Government of Sindh is pleased to declare the minimum rates of the wages @ Rs. 37,000/- per month, for Unskilled Adult and Adolescent Workers employed in all Industrial & Commercial Establishments in Sindh subject to following conditions, with effect from 1° July, 2024."]. یہ اجرت تمام غیر ہنر مند مزدوروں پر لاگو ہوتی ہے جو سندھ کے تمام صنعتی اور تجارتی اداروں میں ملازمت کرتے ہیں۔ 
نوٹ: یہ عمومی قانونی معلومات ہے، قانونی مشورہ نہیں۔ اپنی مخصوص صورتحال کے لیے کسی قابل وکیل سے ملیں یا اپنے صوبائی لیبر ڈیپارٹمنٹ سے رابطہ کریں۔

query: kpk mein minimun wage rate kitna hai
response:
KPK mein minimum wage rate 40,000 rupees per month hai jo 26 working days ke liye hota hai. 
(KPK Minimum Wages Notification, Section: table — "Minimum wage rates: - Monthly rate (26 working days): Rs. 40,000 per month")

Yeh rate sab industrial aur commercial establishments mein laagu hoti hai. 

Note: Yeh general qanooni maloomat hai, legal advice nahi. Apni specific situation ke liye kisi qualified lawyer se milein ya apne provincial Labour Department se rabta karein.

query: punjab mein minimum wage rate kitna hai
response:
Punjab mein minimum wage rate 37,000 rupees per month hai jo unskilled workers ke liye tay ki gayi hai. 

(Minimum Wages Notification, Section: 7 — "UNSKILLED WORKERS (all industrial and commercial establishments in Punjab): - Rs. 37,000/- per month (26 working days)")

Yeh minimum wage rate industrial aur commercial establishments mein kaam karne wale unskilled workers ke liye lagu hoti hai. 

Note: Yeh general qanooni maloomat hai, legal advice nahi. Apni specific situation ke liye kisi qualified lawyer se milein ya apne provincial Labour Department se rabta karein.

query: balochistan mein minimum wage rate kitna hai
response:
Balochistan mein minimum wage rate ka taqsim nahi kiya gaya hai, lekin yeh maloom hai ke Balochistan Minimum Wages Act, 2021 ke tehat minimum wage rates ko notification ke zariye declare kiya jayega.

 
(Balochistan Minimum Wage Notification, Section 1 — "The minimum rates of wages shall apply to all unskilled adult and Juvenile / adolescent workers employed in all Industrial and Commercial establishment of any sort (registered or unregistered) located in the Province of Balochistan.")

 
(Balochistan Minimum Wage Notification, Section 2 — "Now, in exercise of the powers conferred by Section-6(1)(a) of Balochistan Minimum Wages Act, 2021 the Government of Balochistan is pleased to declare that the Minimum rates of Wages for un-skilled adult and juvenile / adolescent workers, employed in all the Industrial / commercial Establishment in Balochistan shall be as given in the schedule appended to the  Minimum rates of Wages with effect from 1* July, 2023.")

Note: Yeh general qanooni maloomat hai, legal advice nahi. Apni specific situation ke liye kisi qualified lawyer se milein ya apne provincial Labour Department se rabta karein.

query: balochistan mein minimum wage rate kitna hai
response:
Balochistan mein minimum wage rate jo unskilled adult aur juvenile/adolescent workers ke liye tay kiya gaya hai, woh Rs. 32,000/- per month hai, jo 26 working days par base hota hai [Balochistan Minimum Wage Notification, Section: schedule — "Un-skilled adult workers employed in Industrial/Commercial Undertakings in Balochistan Province:    - Per Day: Rs. 1,231/-    - Per Month: Rs. 32,000/- (26 working days)"].

Yeh rate sabhi unskilled adult aur juvenile/adolescent workers par lagu hoti hai, jo Balochistan ke industrial aur commercial establishments mein kaam karte hain [Balochistan Minimum Wage Notification, Section: 1 — "The minimum rates of wages shall apply to all unskilled adult and Juvenile / adolescent workers employed in all Industrial and Commercial establishment of any sort (registered or unregistered) located in the Province of Balochistan."].

Yeh minimum wage rate 1st July, 2023 se lagu hai [Balochistan Minimum Wage Notification, Section: schedule — "EFFECTIVE DATE: 1st July, 2023."].

Note: Yeh general qanooni maloomat hai, legal advice nahi. Apni specific situation ke liye kisi qualified lawyer se milein ya apne provincial Labour Department se rabta karein.

Yeh minimum wage sabhi industrial aur commercial establishments par laagu hoti hai, chahe woh registered hon ya unregistered. 
(Balochistan Minimum Wage Notification, Section: 1 — "The minimum rates of wages shall apply to all unskilled adult and Juvenile / adolescent workers employed in all Industrial and Commercial establishment of any sort (registered or unregistered) located in the Province of Balochistan.")

query: kpk mein minimum wage kitni hai
response:
KPK mein minimum wage Rs. 40,000 per month hai. 
(KPK Minimum Wages Notification, Section: table — "Minimum wage rates: - Daily rate (8 hours): Rs. 46 per day - Monthly rate (26 working days): Rs. 40,000 per month")

Yeh minimum wage sabhi adult, unskilled, juvenile aur adolescent workers ke liye hai jo KPK ke industrial aur commercial establishments mein kaam karte hain. 
Note: Yeh general qanooni maloomat hai, legal advice nahi. Apni specific situation ke liye kisi qualified lawyer se milein ya apne provincial Labour Department se rabta karein.

query: balochistan mein minimum wage rate kia hai
response:
Balochistan mein minimum wage rate jo un-skilled adult aur juvenile/adolescent workers ke liye tay ki gayi hai, woh Rs. 32,000/- per month (26 working days) hai, aur per day ki rate Rs. 1,231/- hai [Balochistan Minimum Wage Notification, Section: schedule — "Un-skilled adult workers employed in Industrial/Commercial Undertakings in Balochistan Province:    - Per Day: Rs. 1,231/-    - Per Month: Rs. 32,000/- (26 working days)"]. 

Yeh minimum wage rate sabhi Industrial/Commercial establishments par lagu hoti hai, chahe woh registered hon ya unregistered, aur yeh rate poore Balochistan province mein uniformly lagu hoti hai [Balochistan Minimum Wage Notification, Section: 1 — "The minimum rates of wages shall apply to all unskilled adult and Juvenile / adolescent workers employed in all Industrial and Commercial establishment of any sort (registered or unregistered) located in the Province of Balochistan."]. 

Note: Yeh general qanooni maloomat hai, legal advice nahi. Apni specific situation ke liye kisi qualified lawyer se milein ya apne provincial Labour Department se rabta karein.
